coverage_summary: The improved test cases provide solid validation and boundary coverage for input fields (first name, last name, username, email, phone, password) and include a key security test for account lockout. However, coverage gaps remain: no positive test cases, missing boundary for password minimum length, no confirm password mismatch tests, no security tests for SQL injection or XSS, and no verification of successful registration workflows. Business rule coverage is limited to lockout only.
remaining_gaps: No positive/valid registration test cases (happy path)
Missing boundary test for password minimum length (e.g., 7 characters)
Missing boundary test for maximum allowed length of password (e.g., 20 characters)
No test for confirm password mismatch
No test for empty required fields (first name, last name, username, email, phone, password)
No test for special characters in username (alphanumeric only requirement not covered)
No test for email with valid but complex formats (e.g., subdomain, plus sign)
No security tests for SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), or CSRF
No test for username uniqueness case sensitivity
No test for password history or reuse rules
No test for account lockout with correct credentials before lockout expiry
No test for internationalization (e.g., UTF-8 characters in names)
No test for phone number with leading zeros or country codes (if allowed)
recommendations: Add positive test cases covering successful registration with valid data at all field boundaries (e.g., max length exactly 50 for name, exactly 10 for phone)
Include boundary tests for password length: exactly 8 (minimum), exactly 20 (maximum), 7 and 21 to verify rejection
Add negative tests for confirm password field: mismatch, empty, special characters
Implement tests for empty required fields to verify proper inline error messages
Add security tests: SQL injection in username/email fields, XSS in name fields, and CSRF token validation
Expand business rule coverage: email uniqueness, username case-insensitive uniqueness, password history (if applicable)
Test account lockout scenarios more thoroughly: correct login after lockout period, failed attempts reset after successful login
Validate phone number format with allowed special characters (e.g., dashes, parentheses) if supported
Include edge case for email with subdomain (e.g., user@mail.example.com) and plus addressing (e.g., user+tag@example.com)
